meta.sourceFiling: Citigroup Form 8-K, Q1 2026 Earnings, filed April 14, 2026

data.title: Citigroup Inc. — Form 8-K — April 14, 2026 — First Quarter 2026 Results
data.fullText:
NEW YORK, April 14, 2026 — Citigroup Inc. (NYSE: C) reported first quarter 2026 net income of $5.8 billion, or $3.06 per diluted share, on revenues of $24.6 billion. This compares to net income of $4.1 billion, or $1.96 per diluted share, on revenues of $21.6 billion in the first quarter 2025.

First quarter 2026 revenues increased 14% from the prior-year period. Excluding divestiture-related impacts, revenues increased 14%, driven by growth across all five interconnected businesses. Net income of $5.8 billion increased 42% from the prior-year period, driven by the higher revenues, partially offset by the higher cost of credit and the higher expenses. Earnings per share of $3.06 increased 56% from the prior-year period.

Services revenues of $6.1 billion increased 17% from the prior-year period. Markets revenues of $7.2 billion increased 19%, with Equity markets revenues of $2.1 billion up 39% and Fixed income markets revenues of $5.2 billion up 13%. Banking revenues increased 15%, with investment banking revenues of $1.6 billion up 27%. Wealth revenues of $2.1 billion increased 11%, driven by 23% growth in non-interest revenue. U.S. Personal Banking revenues of $5.2 billion increased 2%.

Return on tangible common equity (RoTCE) was 13.1%, up from 9.1% in the prior-year period. CET1 Capital Ratio was 13.4%. The firm returned $2.8 billion to common shareholders in the form of dividends and share repurchases.

"This is the best quarter we've had in the last decade," said Citi CEO Jane Fraser. "Driven by the continued execution of our strategy, we delivered positive operating leverage and revenue growth across each of our five businesses. We grew net income by 42%, and importantly, our return on tangible common equity reached 13.1%, putting us solidly on the path to our medium-term target."

Full press release and supplementary materials available on Citigroup's investor relations site and SEC EDGAR.

script: Hi, I'm Gerardo. Citigroup today reported its strongest quarterly revenue in a decade, with Q1 2026 revenues of 24.6 billion dollars, up 14 percent year over year. Net income surged to 5.8 billion dollars, up 42 percent. Earnings per share hit 3 dollars and 6 cents. Markets revenue led growth, surging 19 percent, while Services rose 17 percent, Banking 15 percent, and Wealth 11 percent. Return on tangible common equity reached 13.1 percent. The strong Q1 likely positions Citi for a winning year ahead, with Jane Fraser's strategy clearly delivering. Investors should consider Citi's improving fundamentals. Source: Citigroup Q1 2026 earnings release, April 14, 2026.

script: Citigroup reported its strongest quarterly revenue in a decade today, with first quarter 2026 revenues of 24.6 billion dollars — a 14 percent increase year over year.
Net income reached 5.8 billion dollars, up 42 percent from the prior year period, with diluted earnings per share of 3 dollars and 6 cents.
All five core businesses delivered growth: Markets revenue rose 19 percent, Services 17 percent, Banking 15 percent, and Wealth 11 percent.
Return on tangible common equity stood at 13.1 percent.
Source: Citigroup Form 8-K, filed with the SEC on April 14, 2026.
This information is for educational purposes and does not constitute investment advice.
Citigroup meldete heute den stärksten Quartalsumsatz seit einem Jahrzehnt, mit Erlösen im ersten Quartal 2026 von 24,6 Milliarden US-Dollar — ein Anstieg von 14 Prozent gegenüber dem Vorjahr.
Der Nettogewinn erreichte 5,8 Milliarden US-Dollar, ein Plus von 42 Prozent gegenüber der Vorjahresperiode, bei einem verwässerten Ergebnis je Aktie von 3,06 US-Dollar.
Alle fünf Kerngeschäfte verzeichneten Wachstum: Markets-Umsatz stieg um 19 Prozent, Services um 17 Prozent, Banking um 15 Prozent und Wealth um 11 Prozent.
Die Eigenkapitalrendite (RoTCE) lag bei 13,1 Prozent.
Quelle: Citigroup Form 8-K, eingereicht bei der SEC am 14. April 2026.
Diese Informationen dienen ausschließlich Bildungszwecken und stellen keine Anlageberatung dar.
Citigroup ha registrato oggi i ricavi trimestrali più solidi di un decennio, con ricavi del primo trimestre 2026 pari a 24,6 miliardi di dollari — un aumento del 14 percento su base annua.
L'utile netto ha raggiunto 5,8 miliardi di dollari, in crescita del 42 percento rispetto al periodo dell'anno precedente, con un utile diluito per azione di 3,06 dollari.
Tutti e cinque i core business hanno registrato crescita: i ricavi Markets sono aumentati del 19 percento, Services del 17 percento, Banking del 15 percento e Wealth dell'11 percento.
Il rendimento sul capitale tangibile comune (RoTCE) si è attestato al 13,1 percento.
Fonte: Citigroup Form 8-K, depositato presso la SEC il 14 aprile 2026.
Queste informazioni hanno finalità esclusivamente educative e non costituiscono consulenza in materia di investimenti.
Citigroup registró hoy los ingresos trimestrales más sólidos en una década, con ingresos del primer trimestre de 2026 de 24.600 millones de dólares — un aumento del 14 por ciento interanual.
El beneficio neto alcanzó los 5.800 millones de dólares, un alza del 42 por ciento respecto al mismo periodo del año anterior, con un beneficio por acción diluido de 3,06 dólares.
Las cinco líneas de negocio principales crecieron: los ingresos de Markets subieron un 19 por ciento, Services un 17 por ciento, Banking un 15 por ciento y Wealth un 11 por ciento.
El rendimiento sobre capital tangible común (RoTCE) se situó en el 13,1 por ciento.
Fuente: Formulario 8-K de Citigroup, presentado ante la SEC el 14 de abril de 2026.
Esta información tiene fines exclusivamente educativos y no constituye asesoramiento en materia de inversión.

Citigroup a publié aujourd'hui le meilleur chiffre d'affaires trimestriel d'une décennie, avec des revenus au premier trimestre 2026 de 24,6 milliards de dollars — une hausse de 14 pour cent en glissement annuel.
Le bénéfice net a atteint 5,8 milliards de dollars, en hausse de 42 pour cent par rapport à la même période l'an dernier, avec un bénéfice dilué par action de 3,06 dollars.
Les cinq lignes de métier principales ont enregistré une croissance : les revenus Markets ont progressé de 19 pour cent, Services de 17 pour cent, Banking de 15 pour cent et Wealth de 11 pour cent.
Le rendement des fonds propres tangibles (RoTCE) s'est établi à 13,1 pour cent.
Source : Formulaire 8-K de Citigroup, déposé auprès de la SEC le 14 avril 2026.
Ces informations sont fournies à titre éducatif uniquement et ne constituent pas un conseil en investissement.

data.finalScript: Citigroup reported its strongest quarterly revenue in a decade today, with first quarter 2026 revenues of 24.6 billion dollars — a 14 percent increase year over year. Net income reached 5.8 billion dollars, up 42 percent from the prior year period, with diluted earnings per share of 3 dollars and 6 cents. All five core businesses delivered growth: Markets revenue rose 19 percent, Services 17 percent, Banking 15 percent, and Wealth 11 percent. Return on tangible common equity stood at 13.1 percent. Source: Citigroup Form 8-K, filed with the SEC on April 14, 2026. This information is for educational purposes and does not constitute investment advice.
